SPY.name: S&P 500 ETF

analysis.summary: SPY shows a short-term bullish pulse over the next 5 days driven by Powell's dovish labor market commentary, but the model flips bearish on both 30- and 90-day horizons as valuation and macro headwinds reassert themselves. The medium-to-longer-term outlook is dominated by residual inflation above target, elevated rates at 4.453% on the 10Y, and growing signal strength on the bearish side as time horizon extends.

key_drivers.driver: Powell labor market commentary reducing inflation premium
US 10Y yield at 4.453% compressing equity risk premium
CPI YoY at 3.78% — still above Fed target, limiting pivot optionality
VIX at 15.32 in neutral risk regime — no panic bid, muted volatility suppresses downside hedging urgency
DXY neutral at 98.91 — no meaningful FX headwind or tailwind for multinational earnings
Defense/drone technology secular theme (Petraeus commentary) supporting select equity subsectors

base.narrative: SPY rallies modestly in the 5-day window on Powell's benign labor commentary and low volatility, reaching the ~758-760 range, before fading over 30-90 days as sticky inflation keeps rates elevated and earnings multiples compress toward the 806 p50 level by Q3.

bull.narrative: A sustained soft-landing narrative takes hold — CPI prints cool materially, the Fed signals a credible rate cut path, and equity multiples re-rate higher, pushing SPY toward the 826 p90 level on the 90-day horizon with sustained momentum.
bull.catalyst: Surprise CPI deceleration to sub-3% or a formal Fed rate cut with dovish forward guidance

bear.narrative: Inflation re-accelerates, forcing the Fed to hold rates higher for longer; the 10Y yield spikes toward 4.75-5%, VIX breaks above 20, and SPY revisits the lower bound near the 785 p10 level or worse on the 90-day horizon.
bear.catalyst: Hot CPI or NFP print reigniting rate hike fears, or a credit/liquidity event in regional banking or commercial real estate

key_risks: Inflation re-acceleration forcing Fed hawkishness and triggering a de-rating of equity multiples, particularly in rate-sensitive growth sectors
Geopolitical escalation or energy price shock disrupting the soft-landing consensus and rapidly repricing the VIX from neutral to risk-off territory
analysis.conviction_narrative: Conviction is high at 0.9643 across all horizons, reflecting strong model agreement on the directional shift from near-term bullishness to medium-term bearishness, anchored by persistent macro headwinds from elevated real rates and above-target inflation.
analysis.horizon_divergence: Yes — the 5-day forecast is BULLISH (driven by sentiment and low vol), while both the 30-day and 90-day forecasts are BEARISH with rising signal strength (1.78 and 6.56 respectively), indicating that any near-term rally is likely a fading opportunity rather than a trend reversal.
analysis.poly_alignment_note: Polymarket provides no active regime signals or crowd-sourced directional data for SPY at this time, making it impossible to assess crowd alignment with the model's bearish medium-term thesis.

SPY.explanation: SPY shows a short-term bullish pulse over the next 5 days driven by Powell's dovish labor market commentary, but the model flips bearish on both 30- and 90-day horizons as valuation and macro headwinds reassert themselves. The medium-to-longer-term outlook is dominated by residual inflation above target, elevated rates at 4.453% on the 10Y, and growing signal strength on the bearish side as time horizon extends.

TLT.name: 20Y+ Treasury ETF

analysis.summary: TLT shows a near-term (5-day) bullish tilt supported by Powell's dovish labor market commentary, but the 30-day and 90-day forecasts turn bearish as sticky inflation at 3.78% and neutral rate/dollar regimes limit sustained bond rallies. The medium-term path favors modest price erosion toward the low-to-mid $85 range as real yields remain elevated and the Fed holds rates above neutral.

key_drivers.driver: Powell labor market commentary reducing inflation pressure expectations
CPI YoY at 3.78% — well above Fed target, suppressing duration appetite
US 10Y yield at 4.453% — elevated real yield compresses TLT price
Fed funds rate at 3.64% — policy still restrictive, no imminent cut catalyst
VIX at 15.32 — neutral risk regime limits flight-to-safety bond demand
DXY at 98.91 in neutral regime — no strong FX-driven Treasury demand
90-day forecast wide interquartile range ($84.69–$87.17) signals regime uncertainty

base.narrative: TLT drifts modestly higher in the next 5 days on Powell's benign labor commentary, then fades toward $85.50–$85.75 over 30–90 days as inflation remains sticky and the Fed signals a higher-for-longer stance. The neutral macro regime keeps volatility contained and upside capped.

bull.narrative: A meaningful downside surprise in CPI or a rapid deterioration in labor data forces markets to reprice Fed cuts aggressively, pushing TLT above $87 within 90 days as the long end rallies and duration demand returns.
bull.catalyst: CPI print materially below 3.2% or non-farm payrolls contraction triggering recession fears and flight to safety

bear.narrative: Inflation re-accelerates or fiscal supply pressures intensify (e.g., new Treasury issuance announcements), driving the 10Y yield toward 4.7–4.8%, pushing TLT toward $84–$84.70 at the low end of the 90-day distribution.
bear.catalyst: Hot CPI re-acceleration above 4% or a large unexpected Treasury supply announcement widening term premium

key_risks: Inflation re-acceleration reversing Powell's dovish tone and repricing Fed cuts out of the curve entirely
Surge in Treasury supply or fiscal deficit concerns widening term premium and driving 10Y yields sharply higher beyond 4.7%
analysis.conviction_narrative: Conviction is high at 0.89 across all horizons because the macro regime inputs (elevated CPI, positive real yields, neutral VIX and DXY) consistently reinforce the bearish medium-term directional bias, even as the near-term signal is mildly bullish on sentiment.
analysis.horizon_divergence: The 5-day forecast is BULLISH (signal strength 0.003, marginal) while the 30- and 90-day forecasts are BEARISH with growing signal strength (0.0166 and 0.1986 respectively), indicating a fleeting near-term bounce embedded within a deteriorating medium-term trend.
analysis.poly_alignment_note: Polymarket provides no active regime signals for TLT, leaving the model forecast unconfirmed or unchallenged by crowd-sourced macro probability pricing, which neither adds conviction nor raises a contrarian flag.

TLT.explanation: TLT shows a near-term (5-day) bullish tilt supported by Powell's dovish labor market commentary, but the 30-day and 90-day forecasts turn bearish as sticky inflation at 3.78% and neutral rate/dollar regimes limit sustained bond rallies. The medium-term path favors modest price erosion toward the low-to-mid $85 range as real yields remain elevated and the Fed holds rates above neutral.

IEF.name: 7-10Y Treasury ETF

analysis.summary: IEF faces a near-term technical bounce over 5 days driven by Powell's labor market commentary reducing inflation hawkishness, but the 30-90 day outlook turns bearish as real rates remain elevated and the structural supply-demand imbalance in Treasuries persists. The medium-term trajectory points to mild price erosion, with the 30-day bear case reflecting ongoing fiscal concerns and sticky CPI at 3.78% keeping Fed cuts priced conservatively.

key_drivers.driver: Powell labor market commentary reducing inflation premium
CPI YoY at 3.78% constraining Fed easing expectations
US 10Y yield at 4.453% — elevated real yield competing with duration exposure
Fed funds rate at 3.64% — spread to 10Y signals modest term premium, not inversion catalyst
VIX at 15.32 — neutral risk regime limits flight-to-quality bid for Treasuries
DXY at 98.91 — dollar neutral, no strong FX-driven foreign demand impulse for Treasuries
No active Polymarket macro regimes — absence of tail event pricing reduces safe-haven premium

base.narrative: IEF grinds marginally higher over 5 days on Powell dovish tone, then drifts lower over 30-90 days as inflation remains above target, supply pressures from Treasury issuance persist, and the Fed holds rates steady longer than market expects. Price converges near 94.57 at 90 days with modest downside skew.

bull.narrative: A sharp deterioration in labor data or a surprise CPI print well below 3.5% triggers aggressive Fed cut pricing, compressing the 10Y yield below 4.2% and pushing IEF toward the p90 of 95.32 at the 90-day horizon. Risk-off equity spillover accelerates safe-haven flows.
bull.catalyst: Weak NFP print or CPI undershoot triggering repricing of 2025 Fed cut path

bear.narrative: Stubborn inflation readings and robust economic data force markets to price out remaining 2025 cuts entirely, driving the 10Y yield above 4.7% and pressuring IEF toward or below the p10 of 93.98. Fiscal supply shock from Treasury refunding adds convexity pressure.
bear.catalyst: Hot CPI or strong retail sales data combined with Treasury announcing elevated auction sizes

key_risks: Re-acceleration of inflation surprises forcing a hawkish Fed repricing that collapses IEF across all horizons
Treasury supply shock from elevated deficit financing crowding out demand at current yield levels and steepening the curve sharply
analysis.conviction_narrative: Conviction is high at 0.965 because the macro regime inputs — elevated CPI, neutral VIX, dollar neutrality, and a positive but shallow term premium — all consistently point to a bounded, low-volatility range with a mild bearish drift, leaving little ambiguity in directional bias despite the near-term bullish blip.
analysis.horizon_divergence: Yes — the 5-day forecast is BULLISH driven by the Powell labor market signal while the 30- and 90-day forecasts are BEARISH, reflecting a transient dovish catalyst fading against persistent structural headwinds from inflation and fiscal supply.
analysis.poly_alignment_note: Polymarket provides no active regime data for IEF, meaning the crowd offers neither confirmation nor contradiction of the model's bearish medium-term view, leaving the signal entirely model-driven without sentiment overlay.

IEF.explanation: IEF faces a near-term technical bounce over 5 days driven by Powell's labor market commentary reducing inflation hawkishness, but the 30-90 day outlook turns bearish as real rates remain elevated and the structural supply-demand imbalance in Treasuries persists. The medium-term trajectory points to mild price erosion, with the 30-day bear case reflecting ongoing fiscal concerns and sticky CPI at 3.78% keeping Fed cuts priced conservatively.

NG=F.name: Natural Gas

analysis.summary: Natural gas shows a short-term bullish lean over the next 5 days, likely driven by near-term supply tightness reflected in the backwardated futures curve, but the model turns bearish over 30-90 day horizons as seasonal demand headwinds and macro neutrality weigh on prices. The 90-day p10-p90 range of $2.66-$3.72 reflects substantial uncertainty, with the central tendency drifting lower toward $3.19 — a meaningful discount to current spot.

key_drivers.driver: Futures curve in backwardation
5-day model signal bullish but weak (signal strength 0.07)
30-day bearish model signal with rising conviction (signal strength 1.83)
90-day bearish model signal with strongest conviction (signal strength 3.04)
Neutral macro regime — VIX 15.3, dollar neutral, rate neutral
Elevated CPI (3.78%) limiting aggressive Fed easing, reducing industrial demand tailwinds
No active Polymarket regime signals or recent news flow

base.narrative: Natural gas grinds modestly higher in the near term as backwardation supports spot prices and weather-driven demand provides temporary uplift, before rolling over in the 30-90 day window as storage rebuilds seasonally and macro tailwinds remain absent. Price settles near $3.19-$3.35 at the 90-day horizon.

bull.narrative: A sustained cold snap or unexpected supply disruption (LNG export surge, pipeline outage) tightens the physical market, validating backwardation and pushing prices toward the $3.70+ range on the 90-day horizon.
bull.catalyst: Abnormal cold weather event or significant LNG export capacity ramp driving above-seasonal withdrawal pace

bear.narrative: Mild weather, robust domestic production, and softening industrial demand converge to rapidly refill storage, collapsing the backwardation structure and driving prices toward or below the $2.66 p10 level by the 90-day window.
bear.catalyst: Warmer-than-seasonal temperatures combined with record US dry gas production overwhelming demand

key_risks: Weather surprise in either direction remains the dominant tail risk — both extreme cold (bull) and persistent warmth (bear) can overwhelm model signals
LNG export policy shifts or infrastructure outages could rapidly reprice the curve independent of domestic fundamentals
analysis.conviction_narrative: Conviction is moderate at 0.59 across all horizons, reflecting genuine directional disagreement between near-term backwardation (bullish) and medium-to-longer-term model signals (bearish), with no Polymarket regime data or recent news to sharpen the view.
analysis.horizon_divergence: The 5-day outlook is BULLISH while the 90-day outlook is BEARISH, suggesting the current backwardation-driven spot strength is likely transient and will fade as seasonal supply-demand dynamics reassert bearish pressure over the medium term.
analysis.poly_alignment_note: Polymarket provides no active regime signals for natural gas, offering no crowd-based confirmation or contradiction of the model's near-term bullish / medium-term bearish split.

NG=F.explanation: Natural gas shows a short-term bullish lean over the next 5 days, likely driven by near-term supply tightness reflected in the backwardated futures curve, but the model turns bearish over 30-90 day horizons as seasonal demand headwinds and macro neutrality weigh on prices. The 90-day p10-p90 range of $2.66-$3.72 reflects substantial uncertainty, with the central tendency drifting lower toward $3.19 — a meaningful discount to current spot.

HG=F.name: Copper Futures

analysis.summary: Copper shows a near-term bullish setup over 5 days with a modest upside target near $6.37, but the model turns bearish at 30- and 90-day horizons as macro headwinds and elevated rates weigh on industrial demand expectations. The widening 90-day distribution (p10: $6.05, p90: $6.97) signals high regime uncertainty, with the bear case carrying meaningful downside if global growth disappoints.

key_drivers.driver: Global industrial demand outlook
US 10Y yield at 4.45% compressing risk appetite and capex cycles
Dollar neutral regime (DXY ~98.9) — not yet a headwind but vulnerability exists
Flat futures curve signaling no near-term supply/demand imbalance premium
CPI at 3.78% keeping Fed cautious, limiting rate-cut-driven reflation trade
Low VIX (15.32) and neutral risk regime providing tactical stability for short-term bounce
No Polymarket regime catalysts active — absence of event-driven macro support

base.narrative: Copper grinds modestly higher over 5 days on short-covering and neutral risk conditions, then retreats over 30-90 days as sticky US inflation delays Fed easing, the dollar strengthens modestly, and Chinese industrial demand recovery remains underwhelming. Prices oscillate in a $6.25–$6.55 range.

bull.narrative: China announces targeted infrastructure or property-sector stimulus, driving a sharp re-acceleration in copper demand expectations. Combined with dollar softening and a Fed dovish pivot signal, copper breaks above $6.60 and tests $6.97 over 90 days.
bull.catalyst: China fiscal stimulus surprise and/or Fed signals accelerated rate cut path on softer labor data

bear.narrative: Global PMIs deteriorate further, US recession risks re-emerge, and the dollar strengthens above 101 DXY as rate-cut expectations are repriced out. Copper breaks below $6.25 and trends toward the $6.05 p10 level over the 90-day horizon as speculative longs unwind.
bear.catalyst: US recession signal (rising unemployment, contracting ISM Manufacturing) or China data disappointment compounded by renewed dollar strength

key_risks: China demand trajectory remains the dominant binary risk — any further deterioration in property/manufacturing activity could accelerate the bearish 90-day scenario faster than the model prices
Persistent US inflation (CPI above 3.5%) keeping the Fed on hold longer than expected, sustaining high real rates that suppress both growth expectations and commodity risk premiums
analysis.conviction_narrative: Conviction is moderate (0.71) reflecting a coherent directional model signal but tempered by a neutral macro regime backdrop with no clear near-term catalysts and wide 90-day distributional uncertainty of nearly $0.92.
analysis.horizon_divergence: The 5-day BULLISH signal and 90-day BEARISH signal conflict materially — the near-term bounce appears technically driven or sentiment-based, while the medium-term model anticipates fundamental macro deterioration that overwhelms any short-term lift.
analysis.poly_alignment_note: Polymarket has no active regime signals for copper, providing zero crowd-based directional confirmation or contradiction, leaving the model forecast uncorroborated by event-driven probability markets.

HG=F.explanation: Copper shows a near-term bullish setup over 5 days with a modest upside target near $6.37, but the model turns bearish at 30- and 90-day horizons as macro headwinds and elevated rates weigh on industrial demand expectations. The widening 90-day distribution (p10: $6.05, p90: $6.97) signals high regime uncertainty, with the bear case carrying meaningful downside if global growth disappoints.

ZW=F.name: Wheat Futures

analysis.summary: Wheat futures face a near-term technical bounce over the 5-day horizon with prices projected to reach ~610 before rolling over into a bearish medium-to-longer term trend. The 30- and 90-day forecasts both flag downside risk, with the 90-day signal strength notably elevated at 0.91, suggesting meaningful structural selling pressure into Q2-Q3.

key_drivers.driver: 90-day bearish signal strength (0.91) indicating strong momentum decay over the medium term
Flat futures curve signaling absence of supply scarcity premium or speculative backwardation
Dollar neutral regime (DXY ~98.91) providing limited FX tailwind or headwind to USD-denominated wheat
5-day bullish short-term forecast (p50 ~610.35) with tight interquartile range suggesting near-term stability
Elevated CPI (3.78%) keeping real rates relatively high, dampening commodity speculative demand
No active geopolitical or supply-shock catalysts from news or Polymarket regime signals
Neutral VIX/risk regime limiting cross-asset safe-haven flows into agricultural commodities

base.narrative: Wheat grinds modestly higher in the next 5 days toward 610 as short-covering and technical momentum provide a brief tailwind, before resuming a steady bearish trajectory over the 30-90 day window driven by ample global supply, flat carry structure, and no macro catalyst to reverse trend. 90-day p50 settles near 605.

bull.narrative: A weather-driven supply shock — drought in the US Plains, Black Sea disruption, or unexpected export surge from key importers — triggers a rapid repricing above the 90-day p90 of 628, invalidating the bearish medium-term model and sustaining prices above 620 through Q2.
bull.catalyst: Geopolitical escalation in Black Sea corridor or severe drought conditions in US/EU winter wheat belt

bear.narrative: Bearish momentum accelerates beyond model forecasts as global supply conditions remain comfortable, export demand weakens, and a rising USD or risk-off episode pressures commodity complex broadly. Prices breach 580 and test the 90-day p10 of 582 earlier than expected.
bear.catalyst: Stronger-than-expected global harvest data, USD breakout above 101, or demand destruction from key importers

key_risks: Sudden Black Sea/Russian export disruption or escalation in Ukraine conflict reigniting geopolitical risk premium
Unexpected deterioration in US winter wheat crop ratings triggering a technical short squeeze above 625
analysis.conviction_narrative: Conviction is high (0.9363) across all horizons as the model's directional signals are internally consistent — short-term bullish but structurally bearish — reflecting a well-defined regime with strong statistical confidence despite low 5-day signal strength.
analysis.horizon_divergence: The 5-day outlook is BULLISH while the 90-day outlook is BEARISH with a signal strength of 0.91, indicating a classic dead-cat-bounce structure where near-term price stabilization masks deteriorating medium-term fundamentals.
analysis.poly_alignment_note: Polymarket provides no active regime signal for wheat, meaning crowd-sourced probability markets neither confirm nor challenge the model's bearish medium-term thesis, leaving the directional view entirely model-driven.

ZW=F.explanation: Wheat futures face a near-term technical bounce over the 5-day horizon with prices projected to reach ~610 before rolling over into a bearish medium-to-longer term trend. The 30- and 90-day forecasts both flag downside risk, with the 90-day signal strength notably elevated at 0.91, suggesting meaningful structural selling pressure into Q2-Q3.

UVXY.name: ProShares Ultra VIX ETF

analysis.summary: UVXY is expected to see a short-term spike over the next 5 days driven by elevated signal strength, but the 30- and 90-day outlook turns decisively bearish as VIX mean-reversion and structural contango decay reassert themselves. The medium-term path points to significant downside from current levels as the neutral macro regime fails to sustain elevated volatility.

key_drivers.driver: Near-term VIX spike signal with 0.98 signal strength on 5-day horizon
Structural VIX ETF contango decay and daily rebalancing drag
VIX at 15.32 in NEUTRAL regime — below historical stress thresholds, limiting sustained vol expansion
Geopolitical risk in Middle East (Lebanon/Iran deal uncertainty) providing tail-risk support
Dollar and rate regime both neutral, reducing cross-asset spillover into equity volatility
Fed funds at 3.64% with CPI at 3.78% — real rates slightly positive, limiting risk-off panic

base.narrative: UVXY rallies modestly to the $28-29 range over 5 days on short-term vol demand, then fades back toward the low-to-mid $20s over 30 days as the VIX mean-reverts toward 13-14 and contango bleed accelerates in the absence of a macro shock.

bull.narrative: A geopolitical escalation (Iran deal collapse, Middle East conflict widening) or a surprise macro data miss triggers a VIX spike toward 22-25, pushing UVXY into the $35-40 range on the 30-90 day horizon and sustaining elevated vol regimes.
bull.catalyst: Geopolitical escalation or surprise negative macro shock (CPI surge, credit event, or equity flash crash) driving VIX above 25

bear.narrative: Risk sentiment remains benign, geopolitical risks de-escalate, and the Fed signals a credible soft landing, collapsing VIX toward 12. UVXY grinds to the $16-18 range over 90 days as contango decay and low realized vol destroy ETF value systematically.
bear.catalyst: Successful US-Iran deal reducing geopolitical premium, strong macro data confirming soft landing, and Fed pivot narrative solidifying

key_risks: Contango structural decay in leveraged VIX products creates a persistent headwind that can overwhelm even correct directional calls on a 30-90 day horizon
A sudden black swan geopolitical or financial event could cause a violent UVXY spike far beyond the p90 forecast, creating severe losses for short-vol positions
analysis.conviction_narrative: Conviction is high at 0.87 because the 5-day bullish and medium-term bearish signals are internally consistent with well-understood VIX dynamics — short-term demand spike followed by structural decay in a neutral vol regime.
analysis.horizon_divergence: There is a clear directional conflict: the 5-day outlook is BULLISH (p50 ~$28.5) while the 90-day outlook is BEARISH with a wide distribution (p10 ~$16.1, p90 ~$39.9), reflecting near-term vol demand versus longer-term contango erosion and regime mean-reversion.
analysis.poly_alignment_note: Polymarket provides no active regime signal for UVXY, offering no crowd-based confirmation or contradiction of the model's near-term bullish / medium-term bearish divergence.

UVXY.explanation: UVXY is expected to see a short-term spike over the next 5 days driven by elevated signal strength, but the 30- and 90-day outlook turns decisively bearish as VIX mean-reversion and structural contango decay reassert themselves. The medium-term path points to significant downside from current levels as the neutral macro regime fails to sustain elevated volatility.
